Hypothesis: Higher empathy predicts lower moral-judgment scores for harmful decisions.
Same-faculty harm receives lower moral-judgment scores than cross-faculty harm.
Outgroup perpetrators receive lower moral-judgment scores than ingroup perpetrators.
The empathy effect is stronger in outgroup cases than in ingroup cases.
Tobit significant predictors: Personal distress x outgroup perpetrator**; Empathic concern x outgroup perpetrator**
Non-parametric significant predictors: Personal distress x outgroup perpetrator**; Empathic concern x outgroup perpetrator**
